children.label: A
children.text: assess the long-term technical data needs of such systems and subsystems; and

children.label: B
children.text: establish corresponding acquisition strategies that provide for technical data rights needed to sustain such systems and subsystems over their life cycle.

children.label: A
children.text: the development of maintenance capabilities within the Department of Defense; or

children.label: B
children.text: competition for contracts for sustainment of such systems or subsystems.

children.heading: .—Requirements Relating to Assessments and Acquisition Strategies
children.label: b .—Requirements Relating to Assessments and Acquisition Strategies

children.text: be developed before issuance of a contract solicitation for the weapon system or subsystem;
address the merits of including a priced contract option for the future delivery of technical data that were not acquired upon initial contract award;
address the potential for changes in the sustainment plan over the life cycle of the weapon system or subsystem; and

apply to weapon systems and subsystems that are to be supported by performance-based logistics arrangements as well as to weapons systems and subsystems that are to be supported by other sustainment approaches.

children.heading: Preference for Specially Negotiated Licenses.—
children.label: c Preference for Specially Negotiated Licenses.—

children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall, to the maximum extent practicable, negotiate and enter into a contract with a contractor for a specially negotiated license for technical data to support the product support strategy of a major weapon system or subsystem of a major weapon system.

In performing the assessment and developing the corresponding strategy required under subsection (a) for such a system or subsystem, a program manager shall consider the use of specially negotiated licenses to acquire customized technical data appropriate for the particular elements of the product support strategy.
